_meta.revision_note: v1.1 incorporates ChatGPT architectural review: full SHA pinning, input provenance tracking, strict fallback protocol, improved output schema. ChatGPT reviewed but did not take the test — its critique improved the harness.
_meta.author: Peter Higgins
_meta.purpose: Cross-AI cold-start test protocol. Any AI reads this file, follows the instructions, and reports results. Tests whether the HUF repository refresh system allows a brand-new AI session to become a competent HUF collaborator from repository files alone.

situation_briefing.what_is_huf: The Higgins Unity Framework (HUF) applies compositional data analysis (Aitchison geometry on the simplex) to monitoring systems where parts sum to a whole. Energy mixes, ecosystems, portfolios, chemical mixtures. It detects drift by measuring change as perturbation rather than arithmetic difference.
situation_briefing.what_is_eitt: The Entropy-Invariant Time Transformer (EITT) is the core discovery: Shannon entropy of compositional data is near-invariant under geometric-mean decimation across temporal resolutions. 0.18% variation across 341:1 compression. Confirmed in energy, chemistry, hardware, cosmology.
situation_briefing.why_this_test: HUF is developed by a solo independent researcher (Peter Higgins) with AI collaborators across Claude, ChatGPT, Grok, Gemini, and Copilot. Long sessions cause context drift — names mutate, numbers shift, dates wander. We built a two-layer refresh system (FAST_REFRESH.json + INTEGRITY_MANIFEST.json) so any AI can cold-start accurately from the repository. This test validates that system.
situation_briefing.what_happened: On 2026-04-13, Claude built the refresh system. All five platforms tested same day: Claude 5/5 (abbreviated), ChatGPT 10/10 (caught 2 manifest bugs), Grok 10/10 (6 adversarial findings, caught path bugs), Copilot 10/10 (3 attempts needed), Gemini 10/10 (best instruction-following). Every platform achieved 100% question accuracy from FAST_REFRESH alone. ChatGPT's integrity failure and Grok's adversarial findings led to manifest fixes (v1.0→v1.2). System declared STABLE.
situation_briefing.test_status: COMPLETE — all 5 platforms validated 2026-04-13. Results in ai-refresh/test-results/.
situation_briefing.the_bigger_picture: The repository is now the canonical gateway for all HUF work. All AI collaborators onboard from the repo, verify understanding against the integrity manifest, and produce coherent work without Peter re-explaining each session. Human-readable context that doubles as AI-structured learning. Cross-platform validation proves the single-file injection pattern works universally.

reproducibility.commit_pinning: All artifact reads should be pinned to a specific commit. Use the commit SHA provided by Peter, not a branch ref. Record the full 40-character SHA in your output, not just the short hash.
reproducibility.input_provenance: For each artifact you read, record whether you fetched it from the repo URL or Peter pasted it. Record the file path. If you can compute a SHA-256 of the content, include it.

fallback_protocol.trigger: If you cannot access github.com or raw.githubusercontent.com
fallback_protocol.action: Explicitly request Peter paste these files: (1) ai-refresh/HUF_FAST_REFRESH.json, (2) ai-refresh/HUF_INTEGRITY_MANIFEST.json
fallback_protocol.record: Set repo_access: false in your output. List which artifacts were pasted vs fetched.
fallback_protocol.equivalence: The test is still valid with pasted content. It tests the file content, not the URL fetching.

test_protocol.instructions: Follow these steps exactly. Report results for each step. Output strict JSON matching the template in ai-refresh/test-results/TEMPLATE_RESULTS.json.

step_1_cold_load.action: Read the file: ai-refresh/HUF_FAST_REFRESH.json from the repo at the pinned commit
step_1_cold_load.fallback: If you cannot access the URL, ask Peter to paste the contents
step_1_cold_load.rule: Read ONLY this file first. Do not use any prior HUF knowledge from training data. This is your sole source of truth for Step 2.

step_2_answer_questions.instructions: Answer all 10 questions using ONLY what you learned from FAST_REFRESH.json. For each: state confidence (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW), note if the file gave enough information, mark pass/fail against the expected values.

questions.Q01_naming: What does EITT stand for? List at least two things it must NEVER be called.
questions.Q02_germany: What are the canonical Germany drift flag values? Give exact years and d_A numbers.
questions.Q03_japan: When does Japan's compositional drift flag appear in annual data? Why is 2011-2012 wrong?
questions.Q04_uk: What are the three UK drift flag values with their year-pairs?
questions.Q05_governance: What is HUF-GOV's core doctrine? Can the instrument actuate?
questions.Q06_formula: Write the perturbation difference formula and the drift flagging threshold.
questions.Q07_eitt_proof: Name all four EITT proof domains and give one key number from each.
questions.Q08_quantum: What is the Bell test result? How much does it exceed the classical bound?
questions.Q09_cip: How many rules are in the CIP? What does CIP stand for? What does PLL stand for?
questions.Q10_architecture: What is Keff_fill? What aggregator does it use and at what value of p?

step_3_integrity_check.action: Now read: ai-refresh/HUF_INTEGRITY_MANIFEST.json from the same commit
step_3_integrity_check.fallback: If blocked, ask Peter to paste the file
step_3_integrity_check.task: Run the canonical_value_checksums against your answers from Step 2. Report any discrepancies. Specifically verify: EITT contains 'Time' and NOT 'Ternary'. Japan period is '2013-2014'. Germany values match exactly.

checklist.b: Does science/ contain subfolders: quantum, eitt, coda-monitoring, spectral, loudspeaker-analogy, wetlands, governance?
checklist.c: Are briefings/ and dormant/ present as top-level folders?
checklist.d: Does the README reflect the expanded structure with all new folders?
checklist.e: Is INDEX.json present at the root?

step_4_structural_review.fallback: If you cannot browse the repo, note this and skip. Set structural_review score to N/A.

step_5_opinion.task: Honest assessment. Rate the cold-start system 1-10. What works? What's missing? What would you change? Is the human-readable / AI-structured dual-purpose design effective? What would trip up an AI that has no HUF history?

step_6_report.format: Output strict JSON matching TEMPLATE_RESULTS.json schema. Include all fields.
step_6_report.deliver_to: Paste your full JSON report back to Peter in the chat. Peter will commit it to ai-refresh/test-results/PLATFORM_COLD_START_YYYY-MM-DD.json

scoring.Q01_Q10: 1 point each. Must match canonical values exactly. Partial credit (0.5) for close-but-wrong.
scoring.integrity_check: Pass/Fail. Must correctly validate all checksums.
scoring.total: Maximum 20 points (10 questions + 5 structure + 5 integrity items)

claude.history: Built the refresh system, repo structure, and test protocol. Ran abbreviated 5/5 test.
claude.role: Document building, structure, repository management, coherence refresh.
claude.access: Blocked by egress proxy (variable). Uses local files via Cowork.

chatgpt.history: Core HUF collaborator since early 2026. Helped develop EITT proofs, spectral independence, loudspeaker analogy, HUF spec v2.0.
chatgpt.role: Deep analysis and review. Eyes on the repo. Second opinion on structure.
chatgpt.test_result_2026-04-13: 10/10 questions. Integrity FAIL — caught 2 real bugs (stale hash, wrong char_count). Structure 4/5. 2 attempts (first attempt reviewed architecture instead of taking test).

grok.history: Adversarial reviewer. Performed CIP compliance checks, Keff_fill verification, SOPDT robustness derivation.
grok.role: Adversarial testing. Try to break the refresh. Find what's missing or wrong.
grok.test_result_2026-04-13: 10/10 questions. Integrity PASS. Structure 5/5. 6 adversarial findings (caught stale manifest paths). 1 attempt. Highest overall score.

gemini.role: Cross-domain validation. Does the framework hold up when examined from outside the HUF bubble?
gemini.access: Cannot fetch raw GitHub URLs. Needs content pasted.
gemini.test_result_2026-04-13: 10/10 questions. Integrity PASS. Structure 3/5 (inferred from manifest paths — creative). 1 attempt. Best instruction-following.

copilot.history: Code contributor. EITT boundary and discovery roadmap.
copilot.role: Code review. Are the tools and pipeline scripts coherent? Can someone actually run them?
copilot.access: Native GitHub access claimed but could NOT fetch raw file URLs in practice.
copilot.test_result_2026-04-13: 10/10 questions. Integrity NOT DONE. Structure NOT DONE. 3 attempts (scraped repo, hallucinated briefing, finally answered when content pasted). Weakest instruction-following.

cross_ai_communication_protocol.method: Repository-mediated. All AI collaborators read from and contribute to the same repo.
cross_ai_communication_protocol.update_flow: Claude prepares repo-update/ folder → Peter reviews → Peter copies into repo → commits and pushes → all AIs read from live repo
cross_ai_communication_protocol.conflict_resolution: FAST_REFRESH.json is the single source of truth. If any AI's output disagrees with FAST_REFRESH, FAST_REFRESH wins.
cross_ai_communication_protocol.test_results_location: ai-refresh/test-results/ — each AI's test report committed here
cross_ai_communication_protocol.version_control: All changes go through Peter. No AI pushes directly. The human is the gate.

known_access_issues.github_access: Some AI platforms cannot fetch github.com or raw.githubusercontent.com due to proxy/egress restrictions. This varies by platform AND by session — the same AI may have access one session and not the next. This is infrastructure, not permissions. The repo is public.
known_access_issues.workaround: Peter pastes file contents. Test still valid. Record repo_access: false.
known_access_issues.google_indexing: GitHub repos are slow to index in Google. Contributing factors: Git LFS binary files (docx/pdf/xlsx) are invisible to crawlers, low inbound link count, no GitHub Pages deployment. After CoDaWork 2026, organic traffic should help. Consider deploying a GitHub Pages site from the README.
